steps.title: Track Text 2's objection
steps.content: Text 1's claim depends on treating the stone pieces as human-made tools. Text 2 challenges that assumption by saying the pieces may have fractured naturally and that other signs of human activity or DNA have not been found. So Text 2 objects to the inference from stones to early humans in the cave.

steps.title: Reject claims Text 2 never makes
steps.content: Choice A makes Text 2 sound more accepting than it is. Choice C says Text 2 expects Text 1 to be proved correct, which is the opposite of its skepticism. Choice D brings in evidence about Clovis timing that Text 2 does not discuss. The answer is B.
